dialogos.texto_en: Oh, look.\nSeems there's a facility called the 'Sea Turtle House.'
Wow, so there's a separate building\nfrom Churaumi Aquarium?
I also associate sea turtles with Okinawa.\nWhat about you, Uesugi-kun?
Sea turtles, huh...\nI suppose?
By the way, there are places\non the main island of Okinawa\nwhere you can swim with sea turtles.
I've seen sea turtles swimming on TV and the Internet,\nand they look very graceful.\nIt's like they're flying in the air.
Although their movements may look relaxed,\ntheir swimming speed is surprisingly fast.
Huh? How fast are they?
Like, around ten kilometers per hour...
...Is that like how fast jogging is?
Nah, that's like running, more than jogging.
That's quite fast. To keep up with a sea turtle,\nyou would need to swim at that speed, huh?
Well, if you think about it in basic terms,\nthat would be the case.
If you think about it in that way, \nsea turtles may not exactly be the most graceful...
It was just a fantasy.\nWell, the sea turtles would probably be troubled\nif we imposed our human image on them.
For sure...\nIt was only just a thought.
What's up?
They ARE creatures of the sea.\nComparing them to humans\nis a strange thing to do in the first place.
That's right.\nThe environments where we live are different after all.
The sea turtles may be thinking\nabout just how slow and clumsy humans are.
What's up?
They don't always swim fast.\nThere must be moments of elegance.
Their swimming style would surely vary\ndepending on whether they're in a dangerous situation,\nlike being chased by an enemy, or in a safe environment.
Now that you mention it, that's true.\nIt would be exhausting to always swim at full speed.
When we swim with sea turtles,\nwe need to approach them gently\nso as not to be perceived as a threat.
What's up?
If we imagine the scenario where the fisherman \nfrom that one fairy tale, Urashima Taro,\nis riding on a turtle%p-2;――
If the turtle is swimming at a speed of ten\nkilometers per hour, he would be thrown off\ndue to the resistance of the water, right?
That's a fairy tale for a reason.
That's why I said,\nit's just a hypothetical scenario, right?
It's too hypothetical.\nIf we're going to talk about it, we should first ask\nwhether it's even possible to breathe underwater.
...Yeah, you're right.
Well, if we had to come up with a reason...\nMaybe the turtle was considerate of its fisherman rescuer?
Or, perhaps it was just a naturally relaxed turtle\nthat always swam leisurely.
However, it would've taken too long\nto get to their destination, Ryugu Castle.
Yes, he would have turned into an old man\nbefore he arrived, without the magic box that kept him young.
Ah! Maybe there was a warp pad?\nA mechanism, perhaps,\nthat shortened the distance to Ryugu Castle.
If we could create a mechanism like the magic box\nthat speeds up time,\nmaybe we could do that%p-2;――
Wait, why am I even considering this all so seriously...
Teehee!
Kkh! Don't laugh!
No, but%p-2;――
For some reason,\nI thought you were cute just now, Uesugi-kun.
Cute...\nThat's not something you should say to a guy.
R-right!
W-well, if I were to swim with the turtles,\nI'd like to meet a relaxed one too!
Do you really want to swim with the turtles that badly?
Yes, if possible.
I considered it as an option for this trip,\nbut the timing of the tour reservation didn't work out.
That's too bad.
But, I haven't given up.\nEven if it's impossible this time, someday...\nfor sure.
Yeah, I hope that comes true.
